content_en:
Abu Hurairah # related that the Prophet # said, “Allah will say on the Day of Resurrection, ‘O son of Adam, I made you to ride on horses and camels, I made you marry women, and T made you lead and Tarba’ (achieve wealth as well as obedience from others) — so where is (your) gratefulness for that?” (Ahmad)

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations a7

you as you forgot me.’ He 3 will then meet a second (slave) and say, ‘So speak, did | not honor you, make you marry, make you lead, and make you have control over horses and camels? And did J not leave you to lead and Tarba’ (achieve wealth as well as obedience from others)?’ He will say, ‘Yes.’ Allah # will say, ‘Did you realize that you would meet Me?’ He will say, ‘No, O my Lord.’ He 4 will say, ‘I indeed forget you just as you forgot me.’ Then He %& will meet a third (slave) and say the same to him. He (the slave) will say, ‘O my Lord, I believed in You, in Your Book, and in Your Messenger. And I prayed, fasted, and gave charity,’ and he will praise (himself) with the best that he is able to (praise himself with), He will say, ‘Then it is right over here.’ Then it will be said, ‘Now we will send our witness against you.’ He (the man) will say to himself, ‘Who is the one that will testify against me?’ Then his mouth will be sealed, and it will be said to his thigh, to his flesh, and to his bones, and then his thigh, his flesh, and his bones will enunciate his deeds that were (perpetrated by him)...and that is the hypocrite, and that is the one that Allah will be angry with. Then a caller will call out, ‘Let every nation follow what it used to worship.”’ (Muslim) And we will mention the hadith later on in its entirety. Al-Bazzaar related the same from both Abu Hurairah # and Abu Sa’eed %, who both imputed it to the Messenger of Allah #8.

Abu Sa’eed st related that the Messenger of Allah # said, “On the

418 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

Day of Resurrection, the disbeliever will be known by his deeds; then, he will deny and dispute. It will be said, ‘These are your neighbors, all testifying against you.’ He will say, “They have lied’ It will be said, ‘Your family and your kindred (testify against you).’ He will say, ‘They have lied.’ It will be said, ‘Take an oath,’ and they will take an oath. Then Allah will silence them, and their tongues will testify against them, and Allah will make them (ie. the disbelievers) enter the Hellfire.” (Related by Abu Ya’laa)

Hakeem ibn Mu’aawiyah related from his father # that the Prophet 8 said, “You will come on the Day of Resurrection, having Fidaam (blockers, to prevent one from talking) on your mouths. The first to speak from the son of Adam is his thigh and his hand.” Related by Ahmad and Al-Baihagee.

Abu Ayyoob # related that the Messenger of Allah 2% said, “The first to dispute on the Day of Resurrection is a man and his wife. By Allah, her tongue will not speak, but rather her hand and her legs will testify against her for what she used to find fault with in her husband. And his hands and legs will testify as to what he dealt to her. Then a man and her servant will be called in the same manner. Then the people of extravagance (Israaf) will be called. Neither Dawaaneeq nor Qaraareet (a portion of a dinar) will be taken from them, but rather the good deeds of this one will be given over to this one that he wronged. And the sins of this one will be given over to the one who wronged him. Then the haughty, oppressive ones will be brought in hooked rods of iron (to punish them). Then it will be said, ‘Return them to the Hellfire.’ Then I do not know - will they enter it, or is (their situation) as Allah 36 says:

EN A ORER G Ew ti Ks 5}

Kine Gs Crabs 35 15

“There is not one of you bit will pass over it (Hell): this is with your Lord; a decree which must be accomplished. Then We shall save those who used to fear Allah 3% and were dutiful to Him. And We shall leave the Zalimun (polytheists and wrong-doers) therein (humbled) to their knees (in Hell)” (Quran 19: 71,72),

Abu Bakr Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa related this narration.

Book of The End : Great Trials and Tribulations 419

Verse: 40 oh 25 Lo WHI ES app

“That Day it will declare its information (about all that happened over it of good or evil), Because your Lord will inspire it.’

Then the Messenger of Allah 2% said, “Do you know what its information is?’ The Companions said, “Allah and His Messenger know best.” He #% said, “Its information is for it to bear witness upon every male or female slave (of Allah), testifying as to all that was done on its surface, (So) for it to say, ‘He did such and such on such and such day.’ And that is its information.” (Related by Al-Baihaqee) At-Tirmidhee and An-Nasaaee related it through a different chain from Abu Ayyoob #, At-Tirmidhee said, “Hasan Ghareeb sahih.””

Al-Hasan Al-Basree related that Khasfah ‘Amm Al-Farazdagq said, “I went to the Messenger of Allah #8, and I heard him recite this Verse:

“So whosoever does good equal to the weight of an atom (or a small ant) shall see it. And whosoever does evil equal to the weight of an atom (or a small ant) shall see ipl

Then he #% said, ‘By Allah, I do not mind if I do not hear other than it; it is sufficient for me; it is sufficient for me.” (Related by AlBaihaqee)

420 Book of The End : Great Trials and Tribulations
content_id:
Abu Hurairah # meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi # bersabda, "Allah akan berfirman pada Hari Kebangkitan, 'Wahai anak Adam, Aku membiarkanmu menunggang kuda dan unta, Aku membiarkanmu menikahi wanita, dan Aku membiarkanmu memimpin dan Tarba' (mencapai kekayaan sekaligus ketaatan dari orang lain) — maka di mana (rasa) syukurmu atas hal itu?'" (Ahmad)

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 417

kamu sebagaimana kamu melupakanku.' Dia # kemudian akan bertemu seorang (hamba) kedua dan berkata, 'Maka berbicalah, tidakkah Aku memuliakanmu, membiarkanmu menikah, membiarkanmu memimpin, dan membiarkanmu memiliki kendali atas kuda dan unta? Dan tidakkah Aku membiarkanmu memimpin dan Tarba' (mencapai kekayaan sekaligus ketaatan dari orang lain)?' Dia akan berkata, 'Ya.' Allah # akan berfirman, 'Apakah kamu menyadari bahwa kamu akan bertemu-Ku?' Dia akan berkata, 'Tidak, wahai Tuhanku.' Dia # akan berfirman, 'Aku sungguh melupakanmu sebagaimana kamu melupakanku.' Kemudian Dia # akan bertemu (hamba) ketiga dan mengucapkan hal yang sama kepadanya. Dia (hamba) akan berkata, 'Wahai Tuhanku, aku beriman kepada-Mu, kepada Kitab-Mu, dan kepada Rasul-Mu. Dan aku shalat, berpuasa, dan bersedekah,' dan dia akan memuji (dirinya sendiri) dengan yang terbaik yang dia mampu. Dia akan berfirman, 'Kalau begitu, itu ada di sini.' Kemudian akan dikatakan, 'Sekarang kami akan mengutus saksi kami terhadapmu.' Dia (pria itu) akan berkata kepada dirinya sendiri, 'Siapakah orang yang akan bersaksi terhadapku?' Kemudian mulutnya akan disegel, dan akan dikatakan kepada pahanya, kepada dagingnya, dan kepada tulang-tulangnya, kemudian pahanya, dagingnya, dan tulang-tulangnya akan mengucapkan amal-amal yang telah dia lakukan...dan itulah si munafik, dan itulah orang yang Allah akan murka kepadanya. Kemudian seorang penyeru akan berseru, 'Biarlah setiap umat mengikuti apa yang biasa mereka sembah.'" (Muslim) Dan kami akan menyebutkan hadith itu nanti selengkapnya. Al-Bazzaar meriwayatkan hal yang sama dari Abu Hurairah # dan Abu Sa'eed #, keduanya menyandarkannya kepada Rasulullah #.

Abu Sa'eed # meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah # bersabda, "Pada

418 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar

Hari Kebangkitan, orang kafir akan dikenal dari amal-amalnya; kemudian, dia akan menyangkal dan membantah. Akan dikatakan, 'Ini adalah tetangga-tetanggamu, semua bersaksi menentangmu.' Dia akan berkata, 'Mereka telah berbohong.' Akan dikatakan, 'Keluarga dan kaum kerabatmu (bersaksi menentangmu).' Dia akan berkata, 'Mereka telah berbohong.' Akan dikatakan, 'Bersumpahlah,' dan mereka akan bersumpah. Kemudian Allah akan membungkam mereka, dan lidah-lidah mereka akan bersaksi menentang mereka, dan Allah akan membuat mereka (yakni orang-orang kafir) masuk neraka." (Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Ya'laa)

Hakeem ibn Mu'aawiyah meriwayatkan dari ayahnya # bahwa Nabi # bersabda, "Kalian akan datang pada Hari Kebangkitan, memiliki Fidaam (penyumbat, untuk mencegah seseorang berbicara) di mulut kalian. Yang pertama berbicara dari anak Adam adalah pahanya dan tangannya." Diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad dan Al-Baihaqee.

Abu Ayyoob # meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah # bersabda, "Yang pertama berselisih pada Hari Kebangkitan adalah seorang pria dan istrinya. Demi Allah, lidahnya tidak akan berbicara, tetapi tangan dan kakinya akan bersaksi menentangnya tentang apa yang biasa dia cela pada suaminya. Dan tangan serta kakinya akan bersaksi tentang apa yang dia lakukan kepadanya. Kemudian seorang pria dan pembantunya akan dipanggil dengan cara yang sama. Kemudian orang-orang yang berlebih-lebihan (Israaf) akan dipanggil. Tidak ada Dawaneeq maupun Qaraareet (sebagian dinar) yang diambil dari mereka, tetapi amal-amal baik orang ini akan diberikan kepada orang yang telah dia zalimi. Dan dosa-dosa orang ini akan diberikan kepada orang yang menzaliminya. Kemudian orang-orang yang sombong dan zalim akan dibawa dengan tongkat-tongkat besi berkait (untuk menghukum mereka). Kemudian akan dikatakan, 'Kembalikan mereka ke neraka.' Kemudian aku tidak tahu — apakah mereka akan memasukinya, atau apakah (situasi mereka) sebagaimana firman Allah #:

"Tidak seorangpun dari kamu melainkan pasti mendatangi neraka itu. Hal itu bagi Tuhanmu adalah suatu kemestian yang sudah ditetapkan. Kemudian Kami akan menyelamatkan orang-orang yang bertakwa dan membiarkan orang-orang yang zalim di sana dalam keadaan berlutut." (QS. 19: 71-72).

Abu Bakr Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa meriwayatkan narasi ini.

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 419

"Pada hari itu bumi menceritakan beritanya, karena sesungguhnya Tuhanmu telah memerintahkan (yang demikian itu) kepadanya."

Kemudian Rasulullah # bersabda, "Tahukah kalian apa beritanya?" Para sahabat berkata, "Allah dan Rasul-Nya lebih mengetahui." Beliau # bersabda, "Beritanya adalah agar ia bersaksi atas setiap hamba laki-laki atau perempuan (Allah), bersaksi tentang semua yang dilakukan di permukaannya, (Sehingga) untuk mengatakannya, 'Dia melakukan ini dan itu pada hari ini dan itu.' Dan itulah beritanya." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Baihaqee) At-Tirmidhee dan An-Nasaee meriwayatkannya melalui rantai yang berbeda dari Abu Ayyoob #. At-Tirmidhee berkata, "Hasan Gharib sahih."

Al-Hasan Al-Basree meriwayatkan bahwa Khasfah 'Amm Al-Farazdaqq berkata, "Aku pergi menemui Rasulullah #, dan aku mendengar beliau membacakan ayat ini:

"Barangsiapa yang mengerjakan kebaikan seberat zarrahpun, niscaya dia akan melihat (balasan)nya. Dan barangsiapa yang mengerjakan kejahatan seberat zarrahpun, niscaya dia akan melihat (balasan)nya."

Kemudian beliau # bersabda, 'Demi Allah, aku tidak peduli jika aku tidak mendengar selain itu; itu cukup bagiku; itu cukup bagiku.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Baihaqee)

420 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar
content_ar: أبو هريرة # related that النبي ﷺ # said, “الله will say on the Day of البعث, ‘O son of آدم, I made you to ride on horses and camels, I made you marry women, and T made you lead and Tarba’ (achieve الثروة as well as obedience from others) — لذا where is (your) gratefulness for that?” (أحمد) الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations a7 you as you forgot me.’ He 3 will then meet a ثانياً (slave) and say, ‘لذا speak, did | not الشرف you, make you marry, make you lead, and make you have control over horses and camels? And did J not leave you to lead and Tarba’ (achieve الثروة as well as obedience from others)?’ He will say, ‘Yes.’ الله # will say, ‘Did you realize that you would meet Me?’ He will say, ‘No, O my Lord.’ He 4 will say, ‘I indeed forget you just as you forgot me.’ Then He %& will meet a ثالثاً (slave) and say the same to him. He (the slave) will say, ‘O my Lord, I believed in You, in Your الكتاب, and in Your Messenger. And I prayed, fasted, and gave الصدقة,’ and he will praise (himself) with the best that he is able to (praise himself with), He will say, ‘Then it is الحق over here.’ Then it will be said, ‘Now we will send our witness against you.’ He (the man) will say to himself, ‘Who is the one that will testify against me?’ Then his mouth will be sealed, and it will be said to his thigh, to his flesh, and to his bones, and then his thigh, his flesh, and his bones will enunciate his deeds that were (perpetrated by him)...and that is the hypocrite, and that is the one that الله will be angry with. Then a caller will call out, ‘Let every nation follow what it used للعبادة.”’ (مسلم) And we will mention the الحديث later on in its entirety. Al-Bazzaar related the same from both أبو هريرة # and Abu Sa’eed %, who both imputed it to الرسول ﷺ of الله #8. Abu Sa’eed st related that الرسول ﷺ of الله # said, “On the 418 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations Day of البعث, the disbeliever will be known by his deeds; then, he will deny and dispute. It will be said, ‘These are your neighbors, all testifying against you.’ He will say, “They have lied’ It will be said, ‘Your الأسرة and your kindred (testify against you).’ He will say, ‘They have lied.’ It will be said, ‘Take an oath,’ and they will take an oath. Then الله will silence them, and their tongues will testify against them, and الله will make them (ie. the disbelievers) enter the النار.” (Related by Abu Ya’laa) Hakeem ibn Mu’aawiyah related from his الأب # that النبي ﷺ 8 said, “You will come on the Day of البعث, having Fidaam (blockers, to prevent one from talking) on your mouths. The أولاً to speak from the son of آدم is his thigh and his hand.” Related by أحمد and Al-Baihagee. Abu Ayyoob # related that الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% said, “The أولاً to dispute on the Day of البعث is a man and his الزوجة. By الله, her tongue will not speak, but rather her hand and her legs will testify against her for what she used to find fault with in her الزوج. And his hands and legs will testify as to what he dealt to her. Then a man and her servant will be called in the same manner. Then the people of extravagance (Israaf) will be called. Neither Dawaaneeq nor Qaraareet (a portion of a dinar) will be taken from them, but rather the الخير deeds of this one will be given over to this one that he wronged. And the الذنوب of this one will be given over to the one who wronged him. Then the haughty, oppressive ones will be brought in hooked rods of iron (to punish them). Then it will be said, ‘Return them to the النار.’ Then I do not know - will they enter it, or is (their situation) as الله 36 says: EN A ORER G Ew ti Ks 5} Kine Gs Crabs 35 15 “There is not one of you bit will pass over it (النار): this is with your Lord; a decree which must be accomplished. Then We shall save those who used to الخوف الله 3% and were dutiful to Him. And We shall leave the Zalimun (polytheists and الخطأ-doers) therein (humbled) to their knees (in النار)” (القرآن الكريم 19: 71,72), أبو بكر Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa related this narration. الكتاب of The End : Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 419 الآية: 40 oh 25 Lo WHI ES app “That Day it will declare its information (about all that happened over it of الخير or الشر), لأن your Lord will inspire it.’ Then الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% said, “Do you know what its information is?’ The الصحابة said, “الله and His Messenger know best.” He #% said, “Its information is for it to bear witness upon every male or female slave (of الله), testifying as to all that was done on its surface, (لذا) for it to say, ‘He did such and such on such and such day.’ And that is its information.” (Related by Al-Baihaqee) At-Tirmidhee and An-Nasaaee related it through a different chain from Abu Ayyoob #, At-Tirmidhee said, “حسن Ghareeb صحيح.”” Al-حسن Al-Basree related that Khasfah ‘Amm Al-Farazdagq said, “I went to الرسول ﷺ of الله #8, and I heard him recite this الآية: “لذا whosoever does الخير equal to the weight of an atom (or a small ant) shall see it. And whosoever does الشر equal to the weight of an atom (or a small ant) shall see ipl Then he #% said, ‘By الله, I do not العقل إذا I do not hear other than it; it is sufficient for me; it is sufficient for me.” (Related by AlBaihaqee) 420 الكتاب of The End : Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations
